categories.description: Payment, billing, and checkout paths (e.g. Stripe sessions, fee/commission math, promo redemption, webhook entitlement).
coverage_gaps: This template repo ships no payment domain code, so there is nothing to verify here.
A real project would prove: checkout session validation, Connect application_fee math on discounted prices, promo-code resolution before Stripe, and idempotent webhook entitlement grants.
categories.notes: No payment seam exists in this template. Replace with existing_tests once your project adds billing code; never mark PROVEN without a real passing test.

categories.description: Authentication, authorization, tenant isolation, and secret-scanning seams.
coverage_gaps: This template repo ships no auth or multi-tenant domain code to verify.
A real project would prove: signup/invite binding, API scope enforcement, cross-tenant data isolation, and a secret-scanner that fails on committed credentials.
categories.notes: No auth seam exists in this template. Add existing_tests when your project introduces auth; secret scanning should be a deterministic test, not a model judge.

categories.description: Background jobs, queues, cron recovery, and retry/idempotency seams.
coverage_gaps: This template repo ships no async job or cron code to verify.
A real project would prove: queue producer/consumer contracts, cron recovery of stalled work, stale-session reaping, and at-least-once handler idempotency.
categories.notes: No async-jobs seam exists in this template. Map to existing_tests once your project adds a job runner or cron.

categories.description: Yalla pipeline self-tests: proof-contract, test-inventory, outcome-quality, and plan/review coverage evaluators that grade generated artifacts deterministically.
categories.notes: These are the only real tests in the template. They deterministically validate the generated proof/inventory artifacts the harness produces, so this category is genuinely PROVEN by `npm test`.

categories.description: Browser-facing end-to-end journeys (checkout, forms, public pages, mobile, admin).
coverage_gaps: This template repo ships no UI, so there is no browser journey to drive.
A real project would prove core flows with Playwright: checkout, form validation/autofill, public product pages, and mobile responsiveness.
categories.notes: No UI seam exists in this template. Add Playwright specs under tests/ and switch proof_mode to playwright once you have a frontend.

categories.description: Database migrations, template schema, and schema/runtime coupling seams.
coverage_gaps: This template repo ships no database or migrations to verify.
A real project would prove: migration file validity, forward/backward safety, column-writer correctness, and that runtime code matches the migrated schema.
categories.notes: No schema-migration seam exists in this template. Map to existing_tests once your project adds migrations.
